article_items.title: The US government's Anthropic models ban was never about an AI jailbreak | TechCrunch
article_items.description: The Trump administration's decision that forced Anthropic to pull its latest cybersecurity models could be reactionary, retaliatory, or both, but the message is clear: The AI industry isn't immune from U.S. government interference.

article_items.title: Cohere North Mini Code Gives AI Developers More Control
article_items.description: The new North Mini Code from Cohere aims to help developers maintain sovereignty over their tech stacks.
article_items.excerpt: The new North Mini Code from Cohere aims to help developers maintain sovereignty over their tech stacks.
article_items.summary: Cohere’s release of an open model that aims to provide enterprise developers with direct control directly contrasts with frontier AI model providers like OpenAI and Anthropic, whose models are often expensive, deeply controlled by the vendor or restricted, as in the case of Anthropic's Claude Mythos and Project Glasswing . He added that the rapid turnaround of these models from release to release can be disruptive to enterprises that build on them. While sovereign AI has come to commonly mean national or regional control over AI technology and infrastructure, in this aspect, it means control over the model used so that there is more trust and transparency as to how the model is built. Cohere, a Canada-based generative AI vendor, has launched a model designed to help enterprise developers maintain sovereignty, or control, over their AI technology stack. The mixture-of-experts (MoE) model is the AI lab’s first agentic coding model.
